method_atom: A measure of the shared statistical correlation (mutual information / entropy) between two complementary subregions of a spatially extended Gaussian (quadratic/free) system, proven to scale with the size of the codimension-one boundary separating the two subregions (a boundary/area law rather than a bulk law), together with a complete asymptotic expansion of this quantity computed over a domain carrying arbitrary intrinsic spatial curvature.
object_atom: A free scalar quantum field defined on space, partitioned into two spatial regions separated by a hypersurface, in a background geometry with arbitrary purely spatial curvature.
brief_reason: The boundary-scaling law for a correlation/entropy measure between complementary subsystems and its curvature-dependent asymptotic expansion for a Gaussian system is the transferable machinery, while the free scalar quantum field on a curved spatial manifold is the physics-specific object; the two are cleanly separable.
